feed.title: Reinvently — AI News and Technical Guides
feed.subtitle: Independent analysis of enterprise AI adoption, UK AI policy, model evaluation, and AI engineering tools.

entry.title: LLM Benchmark Design: Building the Ed-o-meter
entry.summary: How I built Featherbench, a single-file LLM eval harness running 28 tasks: one variable held constant, refusals counted as failures, and judge bias measured.

entry.title: GLM-5.2 vs Fable 5 vs GPT-5.5: Our 28-Task Eval Results
entry.summary: GPT-5.5 passed 28 of 28 tasks, GLM-5.2 passed 26, and Claude Fable 5 refused to do 5 of them. Full results, costs, and a refusal deep dive.

entry.title: The UK AI Policy Landscape: What Enterprise Leaders Need to Track in 2026
entry.summary: No UK AI Act exists. Instead: sector regulators, a £500m sovereign compute programme, AI Growth Labs and a Hardware Plan. The 2026 map for enterprise leaders.

entry.title: AI Growth Labs: What the UK's Regulatory Sandboxes Mean for Your Sector
entry.summary: The UK's AI Growth Labs open to legal services this summer, with the ICO and three legal regulators involved. What regulatory sandboxes mean for your sector.

entry.title: Is Claude Fable 5 Worth It for Enterprise Coding?
entry.summary: Claude Fable 5 tops FrontierSWE at 90 percent but costs $10/$50 per MTok, double Opus 4.8. Whether the premium is worth it, and when GLM-5.2 is enough.

entry.title: The Agent Governance Gap: $202m Budgets, 26 Percent Cost Visibility
entry.summary: KPMG's Q2 2026 pulse: enterprises plan $202m average AI spend but only 26 percent see costs in real time. Why the agent governance gap decides who gets ROI.

entry.title: How to Sandbox AI Agent Code: Firecracker, OpenSandbox, Docker, SmolVM and nono Compared
entry.summary: Firecracker, OpenSandbox, Docker Sandboxes, SmolVM and nono compared: four isolation models for AI agent code, from 5 MiB microVMs to zero-setup sandboxes.

entry.title: Multi-Agent Orchestration Frameworks Compared: ruflo, Aperant, Sandcastle, Mission Control & Maestro
entry.summary: ruflo, Aperant, Sandcastle, Mission Control and Maestro compared. Why 58,000 GitHub stars measure attention, not reliability, and which tool fits which job.

entry.title: GSD, BMAD, OpenSpec, or GitHub Spec Kit: Choosing the Right AI Development Framework
entry.summary: GSD, BMAD, OpenSpec and GitHub Spec Kit compared: choosing a spec-driven AI development framework, from the 90,000-star Spec Kit to BMAD's $2,000 monthly cost.

entry.title: GitHub Copilot vs Claude Code vs Cursor
entry.summary: GitHub Copilot, Claude Code and Cursor compared on capability, control and cost, with Claude Code at 46 percent most-loved in a survey of 906 developers.

entry.title: GPT-5.5 and the Age of Autonomous Task Completion
entry.summary: GPT-5.5, tested with nearly 200 partners before its 2026 release, completes complex tasks autonomously. The governance questions to answer before deploying it.

entry.title: Claude Memory and Dream: Evidence, Architecture and Risk
entry.summary: An evidence review of Claude Memory and Dream: reported deployment gains, system architecture, and the governance risks of persistent, self-editing agents.

entry.title: UK AI Regulation Tightens: What the ICO Guidance and Parliament's Inquiry Mean for Employers
entry.summary: Three 2026 developments: an ICO consultation, a Commons inquiry and a DRCF agentic AI paper tighten UK rules on AI at work. What employers should audit now.

entry.title: The Enterprise AI Reality Check: Why 79% of Organisations Aren't Seeing ROI
entry.summary: 79 percent of organisations struggle with AI adoption and only 29 percent see ROI. What WRITER's survey and Stanford's playbook say the leaders do differently.

entry.title: UK Sovereign AI: What the Government's £500m Bet Means for Your Organisation
entry.summary: The UK's £500 million Sovereign AI programme, explained: why five companies controlling 70 percent of global AI compute pushed Britain to act on infrastructure.

entry.title: A2A Protocol: An Enterprise Architecture Decision Guide
entry.summary: What the Linux Foundation's A2A protocol standardises, where interoperability still stops, and how to evaluate it in enterprise architecture and procurement.

entry.title: Global AI Adoption: Attitudes and Intensity by Country
entry.summary: Global AI adoption compared: venture funding passed $100 billion in 2024 while the EU leads on regulation. How attitudes and intensity differ by country.

entry.title: Claude Mythos and Project Glasswing: Security Evidence
entry.summary: An evidence review of Claude Mythos and Project Glasswing: reported vulnerability findings, limits of the available data, and implications for security teams.

entry.title: UK AI Companies: A Field Guide to Ten Organisations
entry.summary: A field guide to ten UK AI organisations across research, security, mobility and enterprise software — and what they reveal about the UK ecosystem.

entry.title: RAG vs GraphRAG: A Practical Comparison
entry.summary: RAG vs GraphRAG: how Microsoft's open-source knowledge-graph approach, released in 2024, differs from standard retrieval and when each fits your use case.

entry.title: Microsoft Copilot Studio vs Azure AI Foundry vs AWS Bedrock
entry.summary: Copilot Studio, Microsoft Foundry and AWS Bedrock compared: no-code speed, developer depth or 100+ models via one API. Which one fits your organisation.

entry.title: Closing the UK's AI Skills Gap
entry.summary: 73 percent of UK adults have had no AI training at all. Where the shortage bites hardest, what the £400 billion figure really measures, and the funded routes to close it.

entry.title: Microsoft Foundry: Architecture, Governance and Trade-offs
entry.summary: An architecture and governance guide to Microsoft Foundry: its control plane, model and agent services, Azure advantages, operational costs and trade-offs.

entry.title: The UK's Government AI Institutes
entry.summary: A guide to the UK's public AI institutes, from the Alan Turing Institute to the world's first national AI safety body: what each does and why it matters.

entry.title: Generative AI Adoption by Industry: Use Cases, Data and What Comes Next
entry.summary: Generative AI adoption by industry: 71 percent of organisations now use it regularly, returning $3.70 per $1 invested. The use cases and data, sector by sector.

entry.title: AI-Assisted Web Development: Where It Helps and Fails
entry.summary: An evidence-led guide to AI-assisted web development: where coding and content tools save time, where they create risk, and which review controls matter.
